content.content: {"result":"[{\"name_path\": \"runPrPreview\", \"kind\": \"Constant\", \"relative_path\": \"packages/release/src/cli/pr-preview.ts\", \"body_location\": {\"start_line\": 439, \"end_line\": 549}, \"body\": \"runPrPreview = (options: RunPrPreviewOptions = {}) =>\\n  Effect.gen(function* () {\\n    const git = yield* Git.Git\\n    const config = yield* Api.Config.load()\\n    const packages = yield* Api.Analyzer.Workspace.resolvePackages(config.packages)\\n\\n    if (packages.length === 0) {\\n      return yield* Effect.fail(\\n        new Api.Explorer.ExplorerError({\\n          context: {\\n            detail:\\n              'No packages found. Check release.config.ts `packages` field or ensure the workspace root declares packages.',\\n          },\\n        }),\\n      )\\n    }\\n\\n    const runtime = yield* Api.Explorer.explore()\\n    if (!runtime.github.target) {\\n      return yield* Effect.fail(\\n        new Api.Explorer.ExplorerError({\\n          context: {\\n            detail:\\n              'Could not resolve the GitHub repository target for the connected pull request.',\\n          },\\n        }),\\n      )\\n    }\\n    const pullRequest = yield* Api.Explorer.resolvePullRequest()\\n    if (!pullRequest) {\\n      return yield* Effect.fail(\\n        new Api.Explorer.ExplorerError({\\n          context: {\\n            detail:\\n              'Could not resolve an open pull request for the current branch. Set PR_NUMBER explicitly or open a PR first.',\\n          },\\n        }),\\n      )\\n    }\\n\\n    const token = runtime.github.credentials?.token\\n    if (!token || token.trim() === '') {\\n      return yield* Effect.fail(\\n        new Github.GithubConfigError({\\n          context: {\\n            detail: 'GITHUB_TOKEN is required to evaluate or maintain the release PR preview.',\\n          },\\n        }),\\n      )\\n    }\\n\\n    const tags = yield* git.getTags()\\n    const analysis = yield* Api.Analyzer.analyze({ packages, tags })\\n    const projectedSquashCommit = Api.ProjectedSquashCommit.preview({\\n      actualTitle: pullRequest.title,\\n      impacts: Api.ProjectedSquashCommit.collectScopeImpacts(analysis),\\n    })\\n    const diff = yield* loadPullRequestDiff({\\n      pullRequest,\\n      packages,\\n      required: true,\\n    })\\n\\n    const doctor = yield* buildPreviewDoctorSummary({\\n      config,\\n      analysis,\\n      packages,\\n      pullRequest,\\n      ...(projectedSquashCommit ? { projectedSquashCommit } : {}),\\n      diff: diff ?? { files: [], affectedPackages: [] },\\n      blockingTitleChecks: true,\\n    })\\n\\n    if (options.checkOnly) {\\n      if (doctor.blocking) {\\n        return yield* Effect.fail(\\n          new PreviewBlockingError({\\n            issueNumber: pullRequest.number,\\n          }),\\n        )\\n      }\\n\\n      return {\\n        _tag: 'checked',\\n        issueNumber: pullRequest.number,\\n      } satisfies RunPrPreviewResult\\n    }\\n\\n    const forecast = Api.Forecaster.forecast(analysis, runtime)\\n    const preview = yield* upsertPullRequestPreviewComment({\\n      issueNumber: pullRequest.number,\\n      forecast,\\n      ...(doctor.summary ? { doctor: doctor.summary } : {}),\\n      ...(projectedSquashCommit ? { projectedSquashCommit } : {}),\\n      interactiveChecklist:\\n        (config.publishing.ephemeral ?? { mode: 'manual' as const }).mode !== 'manual',\\n    }).pipe(\\n      Effect.provide(\\n        Github.LiveFetch({\\n          owner: runtime.github.target.owner,\\n          repo: runtime.github.target.repo,\\n          token,\\n        }),\\n      ),\\n    )\\n\\n    return {\\n      _tag: 'updated',\\n      ...preview,\\n    } satisfies RunPrPreviewResult\\n  })\"}]"}

content.content: {"result":"[{\"name_path\": \"buildPreviewDoctorSummary\", \"kind\": \"Constant\", \"relative_path\": \"packages/release/src/cli/pr-preview.ts\", \"body_location\": {\"start_line\": 265, \"end_line\": 437}, \"body\": \"buildPreviewDoctorSummary = (params: {\\n  readonly config: Api.Config.ResolvedConfig\\n  readonly analysis: Api.Analyzer.Models.Analysis\\n  readonly packages: readonly Api.Analyzer.Workspace.Package[]\\n  readonly pullRequest: Github.PullRequest\\n  readonly projectedSquashCommit?: Api.ProjectedSquashCommit.Preview\\n  readonly diff: Api.Lint.Diff\\n  readonly blockingTitleChecks: boolean\\n}) =>\\n  Effect.gen(function* () {\\n    const planAttempt = yield* Api.Planner.ephemeral(params.analysis, {\\n      packages: params.packages,\\n    }).pipe(Effect.result)\\n\\n    if (planAttempt._tag === 'Failure') {\\n      return {\\n        summary: {\\n          lifecycle: 'ephemeral',\\n          rows: [\\n            {\\n              label: 'Ephemeral plan',\\n              status: 'warn',\\n              notes: planAttempt.failure.message,\\n            },\\n          ],\\n          guidance: [],\\n          deferredChecks: [],\\n        } satisfies Api.Commentator.DoctorSummary,\\n        blocking: false,\\n      }\\n    }\\n\\n    const plan = planAttempt.success\\n    const plannedItems = [...plan.releases, ...plan.cascades]\\n    if (plannedItems.length === 0) {\\n      return {\\n        blocking: false,\\n      }\\n    }\\n\\n    const channel = Api.Publishing.resolvePublishChannel(params.config.publishing, plan.lifecycle)\\n    const commentDoctorRules = [\\n      ...previewDoctorRuleIds,\\n      'pr.type.release-kind-match-diff',\\n      ...(params.projectedSquashCommit?.projectedHeader\\n        ? (['pr.projected-squash-commit-sync'] as const)\\n        : []),\\n    ]\\n\\n    const titleSeverity = params.blockingTitleChecks\\n      ? Api.Lint.Error.make({})\\n      : params.config.lint.defaults.severity\\n    const lintConfig = Api.Lint.resolveConfig({\\n      defaults: Api.Lint.RuleDefaults.make({\\n        enabled: params.config.lint.defaults.enabled,\\n        severity: params.config.lint.defaults.severity,\\n      }),\\n      rules: {\\n        ...params.config.lint.rules,\\n        'env.publish-channel-ready': enableRule(params.config, 'env.publish-channel-ready', {\\n          surface: 'preview',\\n        }),\\n        'plan.packages-not-private': enableRule(params.config, 'plan.packages-not-private'),\\n        'plan.packages-license-present': enableRule(params.config, 'plan.packages-license-present'),\\n        'plan.packages-repository-present': enableRule(\\n          params.config,\\n          'plan.packages-repository-present',\\n        ),\\n        'plan.packages-repository-match-canonical': enableRule(\\n          params.config,\\n          'plan.packages-repository-match-canonical',\\n        ),\\n        'plan.versions-unpublished': enableRule(params.config, 'plan.versions-unpublished'),\\n        'plan.tags-unique': enableRule(params.config, 'plan.tags-unique'),\\n        'pr.type.release-kind-match-diff': enableRule(\\n          params.config,\\n          'pr.type.release-kind-match-diff',\\n          {},\\n          {\\n            severity: titleSeverity,\\n          },\\n        ),\\n        ...(params.projectedSquashCommit?.projectedHeader\\n          ? {\\n              'pr.projected-squash-commit-sync': enableRule(\\n                params.config,\\n                'pr.projected-squash-commit-sync',\\n                {\\n                  projectedHeader: params.projectedSquashCommit.projectedHeader,\\n                },\\n                {\\n                  severity: titleSeverity,\\n                },\\n              ),\\n            }\\n          : {}),\\n      },\\n      onlyRules: [...commentDoctorRules],\\n    })\\n\\n    const report = yield* Api.Lint.check({ config: lintConfig }).pipe(\\n      Effect.provide(\\n        Layer.mergeAll(\\n          Layer.succeed(Api.Lint.DiffService, params.diff),\\n          Api.Lint.DefaultGitHubLayer,\\n          Api.Lint.Preconditions.make({\\n            hasOpenPR: true,\\n            hasDiff: params.diff.files.length > 0,\\n            hasReleasePlan: true,\\n            isMonorepo: params.packages.length > 1,\\n          }),\\n          Api.Lint.ReleasePlan.make(\\n            plannedItems.map((item) => ({\\n              packageName: item.package.name,\\n              packagePath: item.package.path,\\n              version: item.nextVersion,\\n            })),\\n          ),\\n          Api.Lint.ReleaseContext.make({\\n            lifecycle: plan.lifecycle,\\n            publishing: params.config.publishing,\\n          }),\\n        ),\\n      ),\\n      Effect.provideService(Api.Lint.MonorepoService, toMonorepo(params.packages)),\\n      Effect.provideService(\\n        Api.Lint.PrService,\\n        yield* Api.Lint.fromPullRequest(params.pullRequest),\\n      ),\\n    )\\n\\n    return {\\n      summary: Api.Commentator.createDoctorSummary(report, {\\n        lifecycle: plan.lifecycle,\\n        plannedPackages: plannedItems.length,\\n        ...(channel.mode === 'manual' && plan.lifecycle === 'ephemeral'\\n          ? {\\n              runbook: {\\n                title: 'Manual Preview Runbook',\\n                commands: [\\n                  ...params.config.operator.prepareCommands,\\n                  `PR_NUMBER=${String(plan.releases.find(Api.Planner.Ephemeral.is)?.prerelease.prNumber ?? params.pullRequest.number)} ${appendReleaseCommand(params.config.operator.releaseCommand, 'plan --lifecycle ephemeral')}`,\\n                  appendReleaseCommand(params.config.operator.releaseCommand, 'doctor'),\\n                  appendReleaseCommand(\\n                    params.config.operator.releaseCommand,\\n                    'apply --yes --tag pr',\\n                  ),\\n                ],\\n                note:\\n                  'Step 2 writes the exact ephemeral publish plan to `.release/plan.json`. ' +\\n                  'Step 4 publishes those packages to the `pr` dist-tag.',\\n              },\\n              deferredChecks: manualPreviewDeferredRules.flatMap((rule) =>\\n                rule.data.preventsDescriptions && rule.data.preventsDescriptions.length > 0\\n                  ? [\\n                      {\\n                        label: rule.data.description,\\n                        ruleId: rule.data.id,\\n                        preventsDescriptions: rule.data.preventsDescriptions,\\n                        checkCommand: appendReleaseCommand(\\n                          params.config.operator.releaseCommand,\\n                          `doctor --onlyRule ${rule.data.id}`,\\n                        ),\\n                      },\\n                    ]\\n                  : [],\\n              ),\\n            }\\n          : {}),\\n      }),\\n      blocking: hasBlockingViolations(report),\\n    }\\n  })\"}]"}

content.thinking:
I can see an important discrepancy. The `runPrPreview` function in the diff shows changes, but the current file body I retrieved doesn't match the diff. Specifically:

From the diff, `runPrPreview` should:
1. Call `loadConfiguredPullRequestDiff` (not `loadPullRequestDiff`) with config and remote
2. Pass `explicitDiffRemote` to `buildPreviewDoctorSummary`
3. Call `loadDiffRemote` to compute `diffRemote`

But the current file shows `runPrPreview` still calling `loadPullRequestDiff` directly without any remote handling, and `buildPreviewDoctorSummary` still without `explicitDiffRemote`.
 
This is strange—the diff clearly shows these additions with the `loadDiffRemote` call and the conditional spread of `explicitDiffRemote`, but the file I'm looking at doesn't have them. Either the file body I retrieved is from before the PR was merged, or something else is off with what I'm seeing. I'm noticing a discrepancy—the diff shows the code should be calling `loadConfiguredPullRequestDiff` with the remote option, but the current file still has the old `loadPullRequestDiff` call. The PR metadata indicates it was merged today, so either the file retrieval is stale or the changes didn't actually get applied despite the merge status. not in the gitStatus modifications).

Wait, looking at gitStatus more carefully, I see files like ` M packages/release/src/api/executor/publish.ts` etc. The files with "M" on the right side are unstaged changes. But `pr-preview.ts` is not listed...

Actually the PR is merged with commit `b854e14937de8e4584737dc37576dfc27055ec50`. This commit is not shown in the recent commits, and `git status` doesn't show these files as modified. This is confusing. Let me re-read:

The gitStatus shows:
```
 M packages/release/src/_.test.ts
 M packages/release/src/api/analyzer/analyze.ts
...
```

These are unstaged changes, but they are different files from the PR files. So the PR was merged but these files have other unstaged modifications.

The `runPrPreview` body I'm seeing might be the current post-merge state, which would mean the diff I was given represents the changes that were actually applied. But the function still shows `loadPullRequestDiff` while the diff claims it was changed to `loadConfiguredPullRequestDiff` — either I'm looking at a cached version or the file hasn't been refreshed yet.

Since the PR merged today and my git status is a snapshot from the conversation start, the information might be stale. The merge happened at `2026-03-18T14:13:21Z`, so there's likely a timing mismatch between when I checked the status and when the merge completed. I'm realizing there's a mismatch between the diff showing changes to the imports and function calls, but the current code I'm seeing uses different function names. Let me refocus on the actual PR review task rather than getting caught up in whether the code is pre- or post-merge. I should evaluate the code quality based on the diff provided, looking for potential issues like dead code or unnecessary changes. So when `options.remote` is provided, both `diffRemote` and the actual diff computation go through `resolveDiffRemote`, keeping them consistent. But when `options.remote` isn't provided, I need to check what happens—the code path diverges and I should verify they're still aligned.

Looking at the actual implementation, I'm noticing a few issues: `loadDiffRemote` is just a thin wrapper around `resolveDiffRemote` that doesn't add any real value, and the conditional spreading pattern for `explicitDiffRemote` means we're computing `diffRemote` but potentially not using it depending on whether the remote was explicitly passed.

The argument parsing in `parseAction` handles the edge case where `--remote` is the last argument correctly by returning `null` from `normalizeRemoteArg`. But there's duplication across the codebase—I'm seeing `normalizeRemoteArg` defined in both `pr-lib.ts` and `pr-preview-diff.ts` (as `normalizeRemoteOverride`), and they're doing essentially the same thing.

The options object construction in `pr.ts` is unnecessarily verbose with all those conditional spreads. Since `RunPrPreviewOptions` has optional fields anyway, passing `{ checkOnly: action.checkOnly, remote: action.remote }` would be functionally equivalent and cleaner than the current approach.

There's also a discrepancy with `buildPreviewDoctorSummary` — the diff shows `explicitDiffRemote` being added to the parameters, but the current code body I'm seeing doesn't have it. This might be a caching issue if the PR is already merged.

Looking at the actual issues, I'm spotting a P2 problem: there's a trivial `loadDiffRemote` wrapper function that just calls `resolveDiffRemote` directly without adding any value.

Another P2 issue is that when `options.remote` is undefined, `diffRemote` gets computed but never actually used, which is wasteful. Similarly, the conditional spread for `remote` in the `loadConfiguredPullRequestDiff` call is unnecessary since the parameter already handles undefined values correctly.

There's also a P1 issue where `runPrPreview` resolves the remote twice—once explicitly with `loadDiffRemote` and again when passing it to `loadConfiguredPullRequestDiff`—creating redundant work.

Another concern is that `parseAction(['preview', '--remote'])` silently returns null when the flag lacks a value, but the help text doesn't document this behavior, so users might be confused by the silent failure instead of getting an error message.

Looking at the diff, `buildPreviewDoctorSummary` now properly accepts the `explicitDiffRemote` parameter...

Wait, but the current file body I'm seeing doesn't actually have `explicitDiffRemote` in that function. That's strange—either the PR changes haven't been applied yet, or there's a mismatch between what the LSP is showing me and what's actually in the codebase.

Looking at the test expectations versus what's in the current file, there are clear differences: the test expects `'bun run release doctor --remote fork'` but the code shows just `appendReleaseCommand(..., 'doctor')` without the remote flag, and the apply command differs too (`apply --yes` vs `apply --yes --tag pr`). The git snapshot appears to be from before the PR was merged, which is making this harder to reconcile.

Let me focus on the actual code review of the PR diff itself. I'm noticing a potential issue with how `runPrPreview` handles the remote parameter—it passes the raw `options.remote` to `loadConfiguredPullRequestDiff`, but then displays the normalized `diffRemote` value. Both paths should be using the same resolution logic through `resolveDiffRemote`, so they should align, but I need to verify this is actually consistent throughout.

Looking at the other issues: `loadDiffRemote` is just a thin wrapper around `resolveDiffRemote` that doesn't add any real value, and the conditional spread syntax for the optional remote parameter is unnecessarily verbose when it could just pass the value directly.

There's also redundant computation happening — `diffRemote` gets calculated even when `options.remote` isn't provided, then immediately discarded. In `pr.ts`, the same pattern shows up with conditional spreading for boolean flags that could just be set directly since undefined fields are equivalent to not being set.

I'm also noticing that `normalizeRemoteArg` and `normalizeRemoteOverride` are doing essentially the same thing across two different files — both trim whitespace and reject empty strings, though one has an extra check for strings starting with `--`. This duplication could lead to inconsistent behavior depending on which code path gets used.

Most of these are lower-priority issues since the core functionality works correctly, but let me dig into the `renderDoctorCommandSuffix` function more carefully. I'm seeing a potential bug where pushing `'--remote fork'` as a single string with a space, then joining with spaces, might not produce the intended command format.

Actually, looking at how `extraArgs` contains compound strings like `"--onlyRule env.git-remote"`, the join operation would still work correctly. The real issue is more of a design inconsistency—the function treats `extraArgs` as individual arguments but each element can actually be a multi-word string, which is a bit of a code smell.

I'm also wondering if `explicitDiffRemote` could contain spaces. If someone passes something like `"my remote"`, the normalization would trim it but not reject it, resulting in a malformed command like `"--remote my remote"`. Though in practice this might not be a real issue depending on what valid remote names are.

Git remote names can't actually contain spaces, so this edge case is unlikely to come up in real usage. But it's worth noting that `normalizeRemoteArg` and `normalizeRemoteOverride` don't validate against this—they accept any non-empty, non-flag-looking string, which means someone could theoretically pass a remote name with spaces and generate an invalid command in the runbook. There's no validation to catch this.

Overall, the PR is functionally correct and delivers the intended feature. The concerns are mostly around code quality—some redundant abstractions and potential edge cases that could be tightened up. I haven't found any actual P0 bugs.

Interesting! The file `pr-lib.ts` doesn't exist in the current codebase. And the symbols overview for `pr-preview.ts` shows:
- `RunPrPreviewOptions` interface only has `checkOnly` property, not `remote`
- `buildPreviewDoctorSummary` params don't include `explicitDiffRemote`
- `loadPullRequestDiff` is still there (not `loadConfiguredPullRequestDiff`)
- `runPrPreview` is there

This clearly shows the PR changes are NOT merged into the working directory yet. The PR was "merged" according to the metadata (mergedAt timestamp exists), but the local working directory doesn't reflect it. This is a code review of a PR.


Now I'm looking at the actual code changes to spot problems. There's a trivial wrapper function that just delegates to another function, a dead assignment where a variable gets computed but never used, duplicate normalization logic spread across two files, and some questionable patterns around optional boolean handling.

content.text:
Based on my analysis of the diff, the files don't exist in the current working directory (the PR is not yet applied locally), confirming this is a forward-looking code review. Let me assess the complete PR.

`★ Insight ─────────────────────────────────────`
The PR uses a layered resolution pattern: CLI override → config → default ("origin"). This "priority waterfall" is a well-established pattern for CLI options. The key question is whether the override is threaded consistently through all code paths that need it.
`─────────────────────────────────────────────────`

input.title: feat(release): add diff remote overrides
input.summary: The PR correctly adds an explicit `--remote` override for `release pr preview` and `release doctor`. The priority waterfall (explicit override → config → "origin") is implemented correctly in `resolveDiffRemote`. Tests are thorough. However, there are several code quality issues: a trivial wrapper function that adds no value, a dead assignment, duplicated normalization logic across two files, and an "args as compound strings with embedded spaces" pattern in `renderDoctorCommandSuffix` that silently allows invalid remote names containing spaces to produce broken runbook commands.

findings.title: `loadDiffRemote` is a trivial wrapper adding no value
findings.body:
The new private helper `loadDiffRemote` is a one-liner that does nothing but delegate to the already-imported `resolveDiffRemote`:

```typescript
const loadDiffRemote = (config: Api.Config.ResolvedConfig, remote?: string): string =>
  resolveDiffRemote(config, remote)
```

This is dead abstraction. Call `resolveDiffRemote(config, options.remote)` directly at the call site in `runPrPreview`. The indirection creates noise and a misleading name difference (`loadDiffRemote` vs `resolveDiffRemote`) that implies different behavior where there is none.

findings.title: Dead assignment when `options.remote` is falsy
findings.body:
In `runPrPreview`:
```typescript
const diffRemote = loadDiffRemote(config, options.remote)  // always computed
// ...
...(options.remote ? { explicitDiffRemote: diffRemote } : {}),  // only used when truthy
```
When `options.remote` is `undefined`, `diffRemote` is computed (resolving through config) but immediately discarded. This is a silent dead assignment. The guard should be moved before the computation:
```typescript
const diffRemote = options.remote ? resolveDiffRemote(config, options.remote) : undefined
// ...
...(diffRemote ? { explicitDiffRemote: diffRemote } : {}),
```
This also eliminates the `loadDiffRemote` wrapper naturally.

findings.title: Duplicated remote normalization logic across two files
findings.body:
There are two separate implementations of remote normalization:

- `normalizeRemoteArg` in `pr-lib.ts` — trims, rejects empty strings, rejects strings starting with `--`
- `normalizeRemoteOverride` in `pr-preview-diff.ts` — trims, rejects empty strings only

They diverge on the `--` prefix check. If the normalization logic changes (e.g., rejecting names with `/` or spaces), only one file gets updated. Extract a single `normalizeRemote` function into `pr-preview-diff.ts` (already has the concept) and import it from `pr-lib.ts`.

findings.title: `renderDoctorCommandSuffix` silently accepts remote names with spaces, producing broken runbook commands
findings.body:
```typescript
args.push(`--remote ${explicitDiffRemote}`)
```
If `explicitDiffRemote` contains a space (e.g., `"my remote"`), the rendered runbook command becomes:
```
bun run release doctor --remote my remote
```
which is shell-invalid. Neither `normalizeRemoteArg` nor `normalizeRemoteOverride` rejects whitespace-containing strings — they only trim leading/trailing spaces. While git remote names cannot contain spaces by git's own validation, the CLI layer should defensively reject such values with an explicit error rather than silently generating a broken command string.

A simple `if (normalized.includes(' ')) return null` in `normalizeRemoteArg` (and its `pr-preview-diff.ts` counterpart) would close this.

findings.title: Unnecessary conditional object spread for optional flag in `pr.ts`
findings.body:
```typescript
action.checkOnly || action.remote
  ? {
      ...(action.checkOnly ? { checkOnly: true } : {}),
      ...(action.remote ? { remote: action.remote } : {}),
    }
  : undefined,
```
Since `RunPrPreviewOptions` has `checkOnly?: boolean` and `remote?: string`, both optional, passing `undefined` values for optional fields is equivalent to omitting them. This simplifies to:
```typescript
{ checkOnly: action.checkOnly || undefined, remote: action.remote }
```
or even just:
```typescript
{ remote: action.remote, ...(action.checkOnly ? { checkOnly: true } : {}) }
```
The outer `|| action.remote ? ... : undefined` guard also becomes unnecessary once `RunPrPreviewOptions` accepts the `remote` field unconditionally.

residual_risks: The `renderDoctorCommandSuffix` function builds command strings by embedding flag-value pairs as single array elements with internal spaces (e.g., `'--remote fork'`), then joining. This pattern is opaque — future contributors may treat each element as a true single-token argument and accidentally introduce multi-word elements that only work because they happen to be joined with the same delimiter. Consider a clearer interface where each flag and value is a separate element.

The `pr-lib.ts` `parseAction` uses a manual index-bumping loop (`index += 1`) to consume `--remote`'s value argument. This pattern is error-prone if more value-taking flags are added later. An argument-parsing library (or even a simple two-pass approach) would be safer as the flag surface grows.

content.text:
`★ Insight ─────────────────────────────────────`
The most subtle issue here is the **compound-string-as-single-arg** pattern in `renderDoctorCommandSuffix` — it pushes `"--remote fork"` as one array element, then joins with spaces. This works only because the join separator matches the embedded separator. It's a form of implicit coupling: the array loses its semantic meaning as "one element = one token," which matters if this array ever gets passed to a `spawn()` call instead of rendered as a display string.

The **dead assignment** (`diffRemote` computed but discarded when `options.remote` is falsy) is a common sign of over-early abstraction — the `loadDiffRemote` wrapper was introduced to name the operation, but it caused the result to be computed unconditionally even though it's only conditionally used.
`─────────────────────────────────────────────────`

result:

This works only because the join separator matches the embedded separator. It's a form of implicit coupling: the array loses its semantic meaning as "one element = one token," which matters if this array ever gets passed to a `spawn()` call instead of rendered as a display string.

The **dead assignment** (`diffRemote` computed but discarded when `options.remote` is falsy) is a common sign of over-early abstraction — the `loadDiffRemote` wrapper was introduced to name the operation, but it caused the result to be computed unconditionally even though it's only conditionally used.
`─────────────────────────────────────────────────`

```typescript

```

```

findings.body:
```typescript
action.checkOnly || action.remote

```

```typescript

This pattern is opaque — future contributors may treat each element as a true single-token argument and accidentally introduce multi-word elements that only work because they happen to be joined with the same delimiter. The `pr-lib.ts` `parseAction` uses a manual index-bumping loop (`index += 1`) to consume `--remote`'s value argument. This pattern is error-prone if more value-taking flags are added later. An argument-parsing library (or even a simple two-pass approach) would be safer as the flag surface grows.
